history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing forward with a wall straight ahead (column C), so moving forward is not possible. To progress safely, I will turn right to face the open area at column D, row 7, which appears to be free of lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing forward at cell D7, which is a floor cell ('.') and safe to move into. The goal is not yet visible, so I will move forward to explore further and potentially reveal more of the map.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward with a floor cell (D7) in front. Since the path ahead is free and safe, I will move forward again to explore more of the map and get closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is still at the bottom-center, facing forward towards cell D7 which is a floor cell ('.') and safe to move into. Moving forward will reveal more of the map ahead. I will move forward again to advance and discover more of the environment.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The immediate cell in front of the agent (D7) is a floor cell, safe to move forward. There is a wall at row 3 blocking further north, but it is uncertain what's beyond. Moving forward will allow the agent to explore further safely and potentially find a free path to the goal, avoiding lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward with the cell ahead (D7) being floor ('.'), allowing safe movement forward. The upper rows are mostly unknown, so moving forward will help uncover more information. There is no immediate threat ahead, so I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
Moving forward again would place the agent on cell D7 which is lava (L), which is deadly. Turning either left or right might reveal safer paths, but the lava is on my left side in row 7 and the wall on my right (column E). Turning right seems to put me facing the wall, so turning left is the better choice to explore safe cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is now facing left, with the cell in front being floor ('.'), which is safe to move forward. The goal 'G' is visible several rows ahead and a few columns to the right. Moving forward will help approach the goal path. The left side is free from lava and walls immediately, so I'll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
